compiled_state.author: reconciler

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
Plan-review PASS (0 blocking, 20 advisory). Remediation of valid advisories,
with call sites verified against the code:

- CORRECTION: `_verdict_manifest` lives at `src/rebar/_commands/transition.py:282`
  (NOT attest.py). Plan-review manifest = `build_manifest` in
  `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py`.
- Git SHA idiom: reuse `src/rebar/signing.py:337 head_sha()` (a `git -C … rev-parse`
  subprocess); resolver co-locates there. NOTE: the advisory's `run_git()` in
  `gitutil.py` does NOT exist in this tree — that finding dismissed as invalid.
- verify_signature typed contract to extend: `VerifySignatureResultOut` in
  `src/rebar/mcp_server.py` + `src/rebar/schemas/verify_signature_result.schema.json`
  (new nullable string field, e.g. `rebar_version`).
- Build hook registration required: `[tool.hatch.build.hooks.custom]` in pyproject +
  `hatch_build.py` at repo root + `artifacts` glob under
  `[tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel]` + `.gitignore` entry — else the baked SHA never
  ships to PyPI wheels (dead fallback).
- `-dirty` rule DEFINED: dirty iff `git -C <dir of rebar.__file__> status --porcelain`
  is non-empty (any staged/modified-tracked/untracked change in the rebar source tree).
- Observability: when SHA resolution falls back live->baked->omit, no live SHA on a
  git checkout should emit a debug log (silent-failure guard).
These are distributed into the three child stories below with file_impact set.

compiled_state.description:
Attestation manifests (plan-review and completion-verifier) should record the
**version of rebar that produced the certification**, for audit/provenance —
*not* for enforcement. `regver` (criteria-registry version) already owns
version-skew invalidation; this stamp is never read by `compute_validity` and
never auto-invalidates an attestation.

## Context

- Signed manifests are built by `build_manifest` (plan-review) and
  `_verdict_manifest` (completion-verifier) in `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py`.
- The manifest already carries a `regver:` line (enforced skew stamp) and, for
  plan-review, `verified_at_sha` (the **target** repo SHA). The new stamp is a
  distinct axis: which **rebar gate code** signed the attestation.
- Package version is **static** (`pyproject.toml` `version = "0.6.0"`, hatchling,
  no VCS) — coarse, so version alone can't answer "what code certified this?".
  Hence version **+ short commit SHA of the gate-code checkout**.

## What gets recorded

One additive manifest line, e.g. `rebar-version: 0.6.0 (gc1a2b3c4-dirty)`:
- **Version**: `rebar.__version__` (static package version).
- **Commit SHA** of the **rebar source (gate code)** checkout — NOT the target
  repo — resolved **live-first, baked-second**:
  1. live git checkout of the rebar source -> `git -C <dir of rebar.__file__>
     rev-parse --short HEAD` (dev/editable: the live truth, wins over any baked
     value which goes stale as you commit);
  2. else -> SHA **baked at build time** into a generated, git-ignored
     `src/rebar/_build_info.py` (PyPI/wheel install);
  3. else -> omit the SHA, keep the version.
- **`-dirty` marker** on the live-checkout path when the working tree has
  uncommitted changes (`git status --porcelain`), so a mid-edit signer can't
  stamp a clean SHA that doesn't match the running gate code. Release builds are
  clean.

## Success Criteria

- An auditor reading a signed attestation can determine the exact rebar version
  and (where a checkout or release build exists) the gate-code commit that
  produced it, across dev/editable and PyPI installs.
- Zero behavior change to gate validity: the stamp never affects claim/close.

## Scope / non-goals

- No enforcement / no auto-invalidation on version mismatch (that stays with
  `regver`).
- Version stays static; do NOT switch to hatch-vcs — only add SHA capture.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `build_manifest` (plan-review) and `_verdict_manifest`
      (completion-verifier) both append a `rebar-version:` line carrying the
      static version + short gate-code commit SHA.
- [ ] A SHA-resolution helper implements live-git-first, build-baked-second,
      omit-last, with a `-dirty` suffix when the live checkout is dirty.
- [ ] A hatchling build hook (`BuildHookInterface`) captures the commit SHA at
      wheel/sdist build into a generated, git-ignored `src/rebar/_build_info.py`;
      absent-git build omits it gracefully.
- [ ] A `manifest_rebar_version()` parser (mirroring `manifest_regver()`)
      extracts the stamp; returns `None` for pre-stamp manifests.
- [ ] `compute_validity` is unchanged and never reads the stamp; old manifests
      without the line still validate byte-identically.
- [ ] The stamp is surfaced in `show` (attestation block) and returned by
      `verify_signature`.
- [ ] Tests: both manifests carry the line; live-checkout resolution (incl.
      `-dirty`); baked fallback for non-git install; graceful omit; parser
      round-trip + pre-stamp `None`; no validity impact.
compiled_state.title: Certifications need to include the version of rebar they were certified against
